commands.review_summary:
The PR rewrites the connected Control UI Overview into an operator dashboard, adds supporting overview CSS, and adjusts overview render tests for connected-state assertions.

PR surface: Source +652, Tests +2. Total +654 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding from source inspection: current main blurs digit runs in Overview recent-session names, while the PR renders the same resolver output raw. I did not run a browser reproduction because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Human follow-up is needed because the contributor must add real behavior proof and maintainers should review the privacy/i18n fixes before merge.

target.title: Improve Control UI overview hierarchy
target.body:
## Summary
- Rework the connected Overview page into an operator-first dashboard with a compact summary strip, Attention, Recent Sessions, Codex Usage, Cron, connector/skill health, and log/event anomaly surfaces above the fold.
- Preserve the existing Gateway Access and Snapshot cards for disconnected/first-run flows, then demote them below operational content once connected.
- Keep the layout data-driven from existing gateway/session/cron/auth/log state with fallbacks for api-key-only auth, missing quota data, no sessions, no cron jobs, and unavailable logs.

## Validation
- CI=true pnpm exec vitest run ui/src/ui/views/overview.render.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/overview.node.test.ts
- CI=true pnpm --dir ui build
- git diff --check

## Implementation notes
- This PR intentionally avoids new Gateway RPCs. Message queue state, update posture, and host-health exceptions are represented only when existing dashboard data can support them.
- Follow-up telemetry could make the dashboard stronger by exposing pending sends/retries, installed-vs-latest version, restart-needed state, and host capacity alerts directly.
target.author: homer-byte
